---
title: "ISSS608 Visual Analytics & Applications Group Project Meeting 2"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
editor: visual
---

# Meeting Minutes

**Date:** June 17, 2026

## Opening

The second meeting was conducted on June 17, 2026. The meeting focused on reviewing the VAST Challenge 2026 Mini-Challenge 1 data structure, preparing the raw data for analysis, identifying feature engineering requirements, and confirming the overall architecture of the Shiny application.

## Present

Kelvin Andikko\
Anabelle Eveleen T

## Agenda for this meeting

Review of MC1 data structure\
Transformation of raw JSON files into structured datasets\
Discussion on feature engineering requirements\
Confirmation of Shiny application architecture\
Delegation of work\
Confirmation of agenda for the next meeting

## Data Preparation and Feature Engineering

1.  The group reviewed the structure of the MC1 dataset, including communication rounds, communication records, participants, communication channels, and the broader environment context surrounding the embargo breach.
2.  The group agreed that the raw JSON files should be transformed into structured datasets to support clearer analysis and easier integration with the Shiny application.
3.  The structured datasets should allow the group to examine communication activity by round, participant, channel, and crisis period.
4.  The group discussed the need to create derived fields that would support the investigation workflow. These include crisis phases, channel risk categories, embargo-sensitive keyword flags, anomaly flags, Judge monitoring status, network edges, and pathway evidence.
5.  The group agreed that the feature engineering process should not only prepare data for visualisation, but also help explain how information moved across agents and channels before the public release.
6.  The group noted that the evidence should be traceable within the application so that users can move from summary observations to specific communication records.

## Shiny Application Architecture

1.  The group confirmed that the final Shiny application should function as an investigation workflow rather than a static dashboard.
2.  The application should guide users from an overview of the crisis toward more detailed evidence on event timing, agent communication, and possible breach pathways.
3.  The group agreed that the application should include interactive filters where appropriate, while still maintaining a clear narrative structure.
4.  The application architecture should support around three to four main visual analytics components that collectively explain the embargo breach.
5.  The group also agreed that each visualisation should contribute to the central question of whether the breach was caused by deliberate leakage or by systemic breakdown under pressure.

## Delegation of Work

Kelvin will continue preparing the Quarto project website, study the transformed data structure, and begin planning the Shiny application interface and workflow.

Anabelle will continue exploring possible visualisations, review the engineered features, and identify which visual outputs are most suitable for explaining communication behaviour and breach progression.

## Agenda for Next Meeting

The next meeting will focus on finalising the main Shiny application tabs, discussing the visual analytics design, and reviewing the first Shiny application prototype.

## Adjournment

The meeting was adjourned after confirming the data preparation direction, feature engineering requirements, Shiny application architecture, task delegation, and agenda for the next meeting.

**Minutes submitted by:**\
Kelvin Andikko

**Approved by:**\
Anabelle Eveleen T
